quoteLast: when the Arabs made their first rush, and it was just ten minutes past three when the enemy was finally repelled and the bugle sounded "Cease firing."
title: Blue Lights: Hot Work in the Soudan
author: Ballantyne, R. M. (Robert Michael), 1825-1894

quoteFirst: "Then if that telephone came at thirteen minutes before three you must have left not earlier than
quoteLast: , and the accident took place at 3:12, you ran the distance--it is actually thirteen miles and a half--in twenty minutes; that is, at the rate of forty miles an hour."
title: Manslaughter
author: Miller, Alice Duer, 1874-1942; Gruger, Frederic Rodrigo, 1871-1953 [Illustrator]

quoteLast: I volunteered a first-aid treatment. I coddled you mentally the way I’d like to do really. You are an easy subject, you poor, scared little chump. But it’s a hard life waiting on a stone bench. At fifteen after three I was selfish enough to give you the wake-up ring. Come, how _did_ you like it?”
title: Damned
author: Dorrance, E. S. (Ethel Smith), 1880-

title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 05, January 1885, No. 4
author: Chautauqua Literary and Scientific Circle; Chautauqua Institution; Flood, Theodore L., 1842- [Editor]

quoteLast: . If you don't answer within eight minutes from now, you are a dead man!"
author: Leblanc, Maurice, 1864-1941; Teixeira de Mattos, Alexander, 1865-1921 [Translator]
